title: Learning About Facial Anatomy On A Trampoline | Episode 4 | Dr Tim Pearce VLOG
filename: Learning About Facial Anatomy On A Trampoline ｜ Episode 4 ｜ Dr Tim Pearce VLOG.en.vtt
excerpt: Look at this cool little boy with his sunglasses in his water and his sunscreen on are you ready for summer yeah he's really for summer in desperate for summer let's go jumping this is how you can learn facial anatomy while you're looking after the kids on the trampoline it's tru…

segments.text: look at this cool little boy with his sunglasses in his water and his sunscreen on are you ready for summer yeah he's really for summer in desperate for summer let's go jumping this is how you can learn facial anatomy while you're looking after the kids on the trampoline it's true you can [Music] right now when you're all out of breath I'll

feel the pulses next facial artery hand flat like this and you can send let's feel it yeah looking for my arteries you can feel them company ones over here [Music] - right in my nasal labial fold over here that's a bit worrying transverse facial very clearly pumping temple after putting your whole hand flat really works very clearly up here super choc leer also very easy to feel I can feel super orbital infraorbital raviel's are good to feel - very clearly in the white lip it's not in the pink it's above my right let's do it more jumping yeah

[Music] find out we're doing so what happened here Xander what happened well dad was looking for his facial artery what did you do Xander took this tool what did you do he worked that scar oh dear well scratched very sad isn't it what we say to dad sorry dad don't leave a two-year-old in the vicinity of garden equipment while you try and shoot a vlog so I learned more than facial anatomy today

sorry dad you see your face yeah hello mr. cool face what'd you do next I had lunch are you hungry yeah okay let's have lunch so learning is much more about experience than okay go inside you lead the way inside time for lunch so learning from a textbook is one thing come inside they're actually feeling and experiencing is a whole different thing it's all about building a mental model in your head that actually comes in your head at the point where you need it so at the point of an injection you should feel as vivid it's just a sense of knowing where everything is and one of the easiest ways to do that is to map out your own face it's a bit like parenting really

parenting really you only learn the lessons the hard way about how much you can leave a tool for three minutes did you have a cat it's a good thing so cute so while your heart is really pounding map out where the arteries are you should be able to see and very easily your carotid then the facial artery coming through a little notch here pounding away if you have you lay your hand flat against your face you can often feel it coursing along towards your nose you should feel it up here and your towards your cheek mine is actually right in my nasolabial fold at the point where it meets my nose and becomes an angular artery laying your finger like that you'll be able to feel that one pulsing away as well super trochlea I can almost always easily feel super orbital you can usually feel a temple artery particularly if you lay your hand flat across the whole of your face you should feel it somewhere over here and then you can feel it coursing up towards obviously towards your temple transverse

facial very easy to feel as well infraorbital and easy to feel usually and the other little ones are a little bit more difficult I can't usually feel my mental artery coming tight you to feel my mental artery this can sometimes feel the sub mental artery I'm gonna go and feed these boys you hungry now look at this chika [Music]
